author: 0xunclebill
content:
Michaels changes seem to have stabilized things. I'm not seeing any big issues anymore. I've updated our `epbs-devnet-0` branch with the latest changes

We're still producing empty payloads but thatll be fixed soon

author: 0xunclebill
content: i did see a random epoch or two with literally 0 attestations. but the network was able to finalize in the next epoch each time that happened

author: tersec
content: if 1 of 5 clients is "working", how strong is the evidence that one client is a correct one? just speaking generally

author: tersec
content: I've run that YAML locally and only LH's blocks show up, but again, just abstractly, in an interop test, my next step would be to check, is Dora using LH as its oracle

author: tersec
content: i.e. is it just LH being compatible with itself

author: tersec
content: or have you looked deeper

author: barnabasbusa
content: Yeah so I ran a few matrix tests, lh was able to interop with lodestar, but nobody else

author: barnabasbusa
content: On every other network lighthouse was able to keep building blocks and didn't get stuck at the fork boundary

author: barnabasbusa
content: where other clients forked off, and some even stopped producing blocks

author: tersec
content: ok, there's some evidence of increased correctness of LH

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah

author: barnabasbusa
content: not 100% confident of course

author: barnabasbusa
content: but based on my initial testing it seems likely that lighthouse is more right than other clients. Lodestar is close second for now
